---
title: "Frostwire/gtk-gnutella help."
date: 2007-12-02
forum: General Help
---

### Post by XanderVincent on 2007-12-02
I have frostwire and gtk gnutella installed. Frostwire ran once, didn't connect though.

Problem is, even though I have java... neither frostwire or gtk gnutella do anything at ALL when i select the icons. It's like nothing is happening.

Help?

---

### Post by billgoldberg on 2007-12-02
Try removing both java and frostwire.

Install java using synaptic. Search for "sun". Check the java 6 box. Install.

Download the .deb file from frostwire.com.

Install it.

If you can't connect:

Go to your home folder. Press cntrl+h. Go to .frostwire. Open up/or create "gnutella.net"

Delete its content. Replace with this: (the whole link adres, NOT the content of the link)
[http://mc3.electronicbox.net/gnutella.net](http://mc3.electronicbox.net/gnutella.net)

Save file.

Restart frostwire. All should work now.

edit: frostwire seems to have some bugs, that happen in linux and windows. Restart frostwire if it happens (takes 10 seconds)

---

### Post by cbiere on 2007-12-04
Forget the URL. billgoldberg, it would be best to edit your post to remove this URL. The domain is now owned by a squatter/scammer who violates the GPL by charging for a LimeWire rip-off without providing the sources.

I've explained what to do, if you have no peer connections here:
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3794687&postcount=6](http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3794687&postcount=6)

---

### Post by Dambrosio on 2007-12-18
